description: A vertical, portrait-orientation 'Labor News' headline card from the U.S. Department of Labor, styled like a press-release/social news graphic. The upper portion is a black-and-white (blue-grey toned) photograph of a classroom or lecture setting: an older grey-haired man in a collared shirt and eyeglasses, seen from behind/side at right, gestures while addressing a group of seated adult students who face him; several students (a woman in front with dark hair, a blonde woman, others) are softly blurred in the background, suggesting a community-college class. The lower portion fades to a dark navy gradient. A faint embossed Department of Labor seal watermark sits at the lower right. A small blue rectangular tab with white italic serif text reads 'Labor News,' accented by three short vertical bars. Below it, large white serif headline text reads: 'US Department of Labor announces availability of $65M in grants to help community colleges increase access to in-demand, high-quality training.' The visual style mimics a polished news-outlet graphic to lend institutional credibility. The messaging promotes a Trump-administration workforce-development funding announcement, framing it as government investment in skilled training. Target audience is general public, community colleges, and workforce stakeholders.
notable_text: Labor News - US Department of Labor announces availability of $65M in grants to help community colleges increase access to in-demand, high-quality training
